text: The country's GDP grew by 3.2% last quarter, beating analyst expectations of 2.8%. Exports rose and unemployment fell to a five-year low.
Scientists have published new research showing that regular exercise reduces the risk of heart disease by up to 35%. The study followed 10,000 participants over ten years.
The government announced a new infrastructure programme that will fund repairs to roads and bridges across the country over the next five years.
Parliament held its weekly session on Tuesday. Members debated proposed changes to environmental legislation. No vote was taken.
The central bank held interest rates steady at 4.5%, citing stable inflation. The decision was widely expected by markets.
The prime minister met with foreign leaders to discuss trade agreements. Talks are expected to continue next month.
Flooding across the northern regions has left thousands homeless. Rescue teams are working around the clock as the death toll rises.
The shocking collapse of the company wiped billions off the stock market in a single day. Furious investors condemned the board's reckless decisions.
A catastrophic explosion at the factory killed 12 workers and injured dozens more. Authorities have launched a crisis investigation into the devastating blast.
Outrage as the government slammed critics who exposed the scandalous cover-up. The radical policy has triggered a brutal crackdown on dissent.
The new hospital wing opened today, providing 200 additional beds for patients. Staff celebrated the long-awaited expansion.
Record numbers of tourists visited the city last summer, boosting the local economy and creating hundreds of new jobs.
The team won the championship in a stunning victory, defeating their rivals 3–0 in front of a sell-out crowd.
Crime rates have fallen for the third consecutive year, according to official statistics released by the Home Office.
The chaotic scenes outside parliament turned violent as protesters clashed with police. Dozens were arrested in the alarming confrontation.
Heavy rain is forecast for the weekend across most of the country. Temperatures will remain below average for the time of year.
The minister announced plans to review the current healthcare funding model. A consultation with stakeholders is expected later this year.
The airline reported a loss of 400 million euros for the financial year, blaming rising fuel costs and reduced passenger numbers.
Local elections are scheduled for May. Candidates from all major parties have begun campaigning across the constituency.
The horrifying attack left the community in shock and despair. Authorities described it as one of the most terrifying incidents in the region's history.
